---
title: "IMPS Cluster Analysis"
author: "Erik Larsen"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    fig_width: 10
    fig_height: 10
  pdf_document:
    fig_width: 10
    fig_height: 10
    latex_engine: xelatex
  md_document:
    variant: gfm
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{IPMS Clustering vignette}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  fig.width = 10,
  fig.height = 10,
  dev = c("png", "pdf"),
  fig.path = "../analysis/R/hSaint/"
)
```

# Overview

This markdown was developed to formally document an ad hoc data analysis pipeline for 
TMEM184B immunoprecipitation tandem mass-spec experiments for 
**Dr. Martha Bhattacharya's lab** at the **University of Arizona** for the
endolysosomal paper under revision (2025-02-28) for the Journal of Cell Science

Until a container is built, the `R version` used was **R 4.3.2** with the
packages installed below

# Set up Environment

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
  cat('# Set up Environment {.tabset .tabset-pills .tabset-fade}\n')
}
```

## Attach Packages

```{r Attach Packages, eval=T, include = T, message = F, warning = F, echo = T}
# data/wrangling
library(tidyverse)
library(Endo)

# clustering
library(dbscan)
library(kernlab)
library(RSpectra)
library(pheatmap)
library(dynamicTreeCut)

# GSEA/viz
library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
library(ReactomePA)
library(AnnotationDbi)

# progress bar
library(progressr)

# parallelization
library(future)
library(future.apply)

# sub-module/network graphs
library(igraph)
library(visNetwork)
```

## Load the Proteomics TSCs

```{r Load the IPMS hierarchical SAINT results, eval = T, include = T, message = F, warning = F, echo = T}
IPMS_counts <- Endo::IPMS_counts |> 
  dplyr::select(-1) |> 
  dplyr::rename_with(
    ~Endo::IPMS_counts |> 
      dplyr::select(-1) |> 
      dplyr::slice(1) |> 
      unlist() |> 
      as.character()
  ) |> 
  dplyr::slice(-1) |> 
  dplyr::select(-dplyr::contains("407")) |> # remove extra samples
  dplyr::rename(Protein = 1, AN = 2, MW = 3) |> # convert column names shorter
  dplyr::mutate(Protein = stringr::str_extract(Protein, pattern = 'GN=[[:alnum:]]+') %>%
                  gsub(., pattern = 'GN\\=', replacement = '')) |> # mutate HUGO symbols
  dplyr::mutate(
        dplyr::across(dplyr::contains("_"), ~dplyr::if_else(.x == '?', '0', .x)),
        dplyr::across(dplyr::contains("_"), ~dplyr::if_else(.x == '', '0', .x)),
        dplyr::across(dplyr::contains("_"), ~as.numeric(.x)
                      ) # replace ?'s, empty columns with 0's; convert to numeric
        ) |> 
      dplyr::filter(
        !grepl(AN, pattern = "DECOY")
        ) |> # filter decoy proteins out
      
      dplyr::rename_with(
        ~gsub(.x, pattern = '[0-9]+_', replacement = '')
        ) |> # remove _'s from column names
      
      dplyr::rename_with(
        ~gsub(.x, pattern = '^m', replacement = '')
        ) |> # remove m's from column names (mTMEM)
      
      dplyr::rename(BirAV5_1 = `BirA-V1`, 
                    BirAV5_2 = `BirA-V2`,
                    TMEMV5_1 = `TMEM-V1`,
                    TMEMV5_2 = `TMEM-V2`
                    ) |> 
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(Protein))

  # keep MW and AN for future mapping if necessary
protein_map <- IPMS_counts |> 
  dplyr::select(Protein, AN, MW) |> 
  dplyr::mutate(MW = gsub(MW, pattern = ' kDa', replacement = '') |> as.numeric())

  # keep the df for matrix creation
IPMS_counts <- IPMS_counts |> 
  dplyr::select(-AN, -MW)

  # sum the proteins that have multiple accessions for the same symbol
IPMS_counts <- IPMS_counts |> 
  dplyr::group_by(Protein) |> 
  dplyr::summarize(dplyr::across(dplyr::everything(), sum))

head(IPMS_counts)
```

## Load the Hierarchical SAINT Posterior Probabilities

```{r Load the Hierarchical SAINT results}
hSAINT <- readr::read_csv('../../analysis/python/hierarchical_results.csv', col_names = T) |> 
  dplyr::group_by(Protein) |> 
  dplyr::summarize(score = mean(gamma3)) |> 
  dplyr::arrange(desc(score))
```

```{r Add Empirical Flags}
hSAINT <- hSAINT |> 
  dplyr::mutate(
    lit_flag = dplyr::case_when(
      grepl(Protein,
            pattern = 'AHNAK|ALB|ANXA(1|2)|ASS1|ATP5|CFL1|CLTC|CSDA|EDARRAD|ENO|FARS(A|B)|FASN|FKSG30|GAPDH|GFAP|ILF(2|3)|LDHA|LGALS(1|3)|PCB(1|2)|PDIA6|PHB|PHB2|POTE2|PPIA|PRDX(1|2|3|4)|SERPIN(H1|A11)|SLC25A|TUFM|TXN|UBA52') ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ 0), # Mulcahy contams (2008 paper)
    lit_flag = dplyr::case_when(
      lit_flag == 1 ~ 1,
      grepl(Protein,
            pattern = 'EEF1|EIF|HNRN|HSPA|KRT|TUB(A|B)|RPL|HIST2|H2A|RRP|(X|I)PO|NUP|DNAJ') ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ 0), # Dunham contams (2008 paper)
    CRAPome_flag = dplyr::case_when(Protein %in% c(
      Endo::CRAPome_results |>
        dplyr::filter(!grepl(Mapped, pattern = '/|identifier')) |>
        dplyr::select(1:7) |>
        dplyr::rename(User_input = User,
                      Mapped_Gene_Symbol = Input,
                      Num_Exp_Found = Mapped,
                      Num_Exp_Total = Symbol,
                      Avg_SC = Num,
                      Max_SC = of) |>
        dplyr::mutate(dplyr::across(c(3,5:7), ~as.numeric(.x))) |>
        dplyr::select(-Gene) |> 
        dplyr::filter(Num_Exp_Found > 35.8) |> # greater than 5% of experiments
        dplyr::select(Mapped_Gene_Symbol) |>
        unlist() |>
        as.character() |> 
        sort()
      ) ~ 1, TRUE ~ 0)
  )
```


## Transform Data

```{r transform data}
## re-shape 
IPMS_mat <- as.matrix(IPMS_counts[,-1])
rownames(IPMS_mat) <- IPMS_counts |> 
  dplyr::select(1) |> unlist() |> as.character()

# remove all zero's rows
IPMS_mat <- IPMS_mat[rowSums(IPMS_mat) > 0 , , drop = FALSE]
# remove all zero variance rows
IPMS_mat <- IPMS_mat[apply(IPMS_mat, 1, sd)> 0, , drop = FALSE]

# log transformed
IPMS_mat_log <- log1p(IPMS_mat)

# Z-transformed (confirming clusters are insensitive to large counts)
IPMS_mat_Z <- t(scale(t(IPMS_mat)))
```

# Clustering

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
  cat('# Clustering {.tabset .tabset-pills .tabset-fade}\n')
}
```

## Hierarchical Clustering

### Raw Counts

```{r Hierarchical Clustering raw}
d <- stats::as.dist(1 - stats::cor(t(IPMS_mat), use = 'pairwise.complete.obs'))
hc <- stats::hclust(d, method = "average")
```

```{r Grid Search Cluster Size}
sizes <- c(20, 30, 50, 80, 100)
results <- list()

for (m in sizes) {
  cl <- dynamicTreeCut::cutreeDynamic(
    dendro = hc,
    distM = as.matrix(d),
    deepSplit = 1,
    pamRespectsDendro = TRUE,
    minClusterSize = m
  )
  results[[as.character(m)]] <- cl
  cat("minClusterSize =", m, "→", length(table(cl)), "modules\n")
}
rm(list = c('cl', 'm', 'sizes', 'results'))
```

  + `optimal minimum cluster size` is **50** (elbow; diminishing returns after)
  
  + `# modules` = **28**

```{r Clusters on Raw Counts}
clusters_opt <- dynamicTreeCut::cutreeDynamic(hc,
                                              distM = as.matrix(d),
                                              deepSplit = 1,
                                              pamRespectsDendro = T,
                                              minClusterSize = 50)
names(clusters_opt) <- rownames(IPMS_mat)
```
```{r Get Modules}
module_ids <- sort(unique(clusters_opt))
module_colors <- stats::setNames(
  grDevices::rainbow(length(module_ids), s = 0.6, v = 0.9),
  module_ids
)

annotation_colors <- list(Module = module_colors)
```

Sparse data is too difficult to discern from a global view

  -> Z transform the raw counts **after clustering** to plot
  
```{r RawZ scale}
IPMS_z <- t(scale(t(IPMS_mat)))
colnames(IPMS_z) <- colnames(IPMS_mat)

condition <- sub('_.*', '', colnames(IPMS_mat))

col_order <- unlist(
  lapply(
    split(seq_along(condition), condition), function(idx) {
      submat <- IPMS_z[, idx, drop = FALSE]
      hc_sub <- stats::hclust(stats::dist(t(submat)), method = 'ward.D2')
      idx[hc_sub$order]
    }))

IPMS_z_ordered <- IPMS_z[, col_order]

rm(list = c('condition', 'col_order'))
```

## Module Descriptions

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
  cat('## Module Descriptions {.tabset .tabset-pills .tabset-fade}\n')
}
```

### Hierarchical Raw Counts

Extract gene names from the hierarchically clustered modules and convert to a dataframe

```{r Extract Gene Names from Modules}
modules_list_idx <- lapply(sort(unique(clusters_opt)), function(m) {
  which(clusters_opt == m)
})
names(modules_list_idx) <- sort(unique(clusters_opt))

modules_list <- lapply(sort(unique(clusters_opt)), function(m) {
  rownames(IPMS_mat)[clusters_opt == m]
})
names(modules_list) <- sort(unique(clusters_opt)) |> as.character()

modules_df <- modules_list %>%
  purrr::map_df(., .f = as.data.frame, .id = 'module') |> 
  dplyr::rename(Protein = 2) |> 
  dplyr::relocate(Protein, .before = 1)
```

Include score summaries (mean and median posterior probability scores, ranks, and literature or CRAPome contamination rate)

```{r Summarize SAINT Scores and Contamination Rates}
module_scores <- modules_df |>  
  dplyr::inner_join(
    hSAINT
  ) |> 
  dplyr::arrange(desc(score)) |> 
  dplyr::group_by(module) |> 
  dplyr::summarize(mean_score = mean(score),
                   median_score = stats::median(score),
                   score_mass = sum(score),
                   n = dplyr::n(),
                   lit_flag_perc = (sum(lit_flag)/n)*100,
                   CRAPome_flag_perc = (sum(CRAPome_flag)/n)*100) |> 
  dplyr::arrange(desc(mean_score)) |> 
  dplyr::mutate(mean_rank = dplyr::row_number()) |> 
  dplyr::arrange(desc(median_score)) |> 
  dplyr::mutate(median_rank = dplyr::row_number()) |> 
  dplyr::arrange(desc(score_mass)) |> 
  dplyr::mutate(total_rank = dplyr::row_number()) |> 
  dplyr::arrange(mean_rank)
```

Join the module scores to the modules (per protein) dataframe and the `hSAINT` results for classification

```{r Join the Summary dataframe to the Protein Level dataframes}
modules_df <- modules_df |> 
  dplyr::inner_join(
    hSAINT
  ) |> 
  dplyr::full_join(
    module_scores |> 
      dplyr::ungroup()
  ) |> 
  dplyr::mutate(
    SAINT_classification = dplyr::case_when(mean_rank <= 5 ~ 'High SAINT',
                                            mean_rank > 5 ~ 'Low SAINT'
                                            ),
    
    contams_classification = dplyr::case_when(lit_flag_perc > 5 ~'High Contaminants',
                                              TRUE ~ 'Low Contaminants')) |> 
  dplyr::mutate(classification = paste0(SAINT_classification,
                                        ", ",
                                        contams_classification)) |> 
  dplyr::select(-SAINT_classification,
                -contams_classification) |> 
  dplyr::relocate(classification, .after = module)
```

### Gene Set Enrichment Analysis

Gather `EntrezIDs` for `KEGG` and `Reactome`

```{r Downstream Analysis}
EntrezIDs <- AnnotationDbi::select(
  org.Hs.eg.db,
  keys = modules_df |> dplyr::distinct(Protein) |> dplyr::pull(),
  columns = 'ENTREZID',
  keytype = 'SYMBOL'
) |> 
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(2))

EntrezMap <- stats::setNames(EntrezIDs$ENTREZID, EntrezIDs$SYMBOL)
```

Create enrichment functions

```{r Create Enrichment Functions}
background_genes <- modules_df |> 
  dplyr::distinct(Protein) |> 
  dplyr::pull()

get_GO <- function(genes, ontology) {
  clusterProfiler::enrichGO(
    gene = genes,
    universe = background_genes,
    OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db,
    keyType = 'SYMBOL',
    ont = ontology,
    pAdjustMethod = 'BH',
    qvalueCutoff = 0.05,
    readable = T
  )
}

get_kegg <- function(genes, EntrezMap) {
  entrez <- EntrezMap[genes]
  entrez <- entrez[!is.na(entrez)]
  if (length(entrez)==0) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  clusterProfiler::enrichKEGG(
    gene = entrez,
    organism = 'hsa',
    pAdjustMethod = 'BH',
    qvalueCutoff = 0.05
  )
}

get_reactome <- function(genes, EntrezMap) {
  entrez <- EntrezMap[genes]
  entrez <- entrez[!is.na(entrez)]
  if (length(entrez)==0) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  ReactomePA::enrichPathway(
    gene = entrez,
    organism = 'human',
    pAdjustMethod = 'BH',
    qvalueCutoff = 0.05,
    readable = T
  )
}
```

Run the functions on the dataset in parallel (multiple CPUs)

```{r Parallelize Enrichment}
future::plan(future::multisession, workers = 6)
progressr::handlers(progressr::handler_txtprogressbar())

module_ids <- sort(unique(modules_df$module))

progressr::with_progress({
  p <- progressr::progressor(steps = length(module_ids))

  enrichment_results <- future.apply::future_lapply(
    module_ids,
    function(mod) {

      # tick the progress bar
      p(sprintf("Module %s", mod))

      genes <- modules_df |>
        dplyr::filter(module == mod) |>
        dplyr::distinct(Protein) |>
        dplyr::pull(Protein)

      list(
        module = mod,
        go_bp  = get_GO(genes, ontology = "BP"),
        go_cc  = get_GO(genes, ontology = "CC"),
        go_mf  = get_GO(genes, ontology = "MF")
        # add KEGG/Reactome later
      )
    }
  )
})

names(enrichment_results) <- module_ids
rm(p)
```

Gather enrichment results

```{r Consolidate Enrichment Results}
global_enrichment_results_df <- lapply(enrichment_results, function(x) {
  x %>% 
    purrr::map_df(., .f = as.data.frame, .id = 'GO_Category') |> 
    dplyr::rename(module = 2, GO_Term = Description, GO_ID = ID) |> 
    tidyr::fill(module, .direction = 'down') |> 
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(GO_ID)) |> 
    dplyr::mutate(GO_Category = gsub(GO_Category,
                                     pattern = 'go_',
                                     replacement = '') %>% 
                    stringr::str_to_upper(.)) |> 
    tibble::remove_rownames() |> 
    dplyr::distinct()
  }
) %>%
  purrr::map_df(., .f = as.data.frame)

modules_df |> 
  dplyr::filter(grepl(classification, pattern = 'High SAINT, Low Con')) |> 
  dplyr::distinct(module) |> 
  dplyr::pull()

global_enrichment_results_df |> 
  dplyr::group_by(module) |> 
  dplyr::summarize(n = dplyr::n()) |> 
  dplyr::arrange(desc(n))

# global_enrichment_results_df |> 
#   dplyr::inner_join(
#     module_scores
#   ) |> 
#   dplyr::relocate(c(dplyr::contains("_score"), dplyr::contains("perc"), n, Count), .after = module) |> 
#   dplyr::relocate(GO_ID, .before = mean_rank) |> 
#   dplyr::group_by(module) |> 
#   dplyr::arrange(desc(mean_score), p.adjust)
#   dplyr::filter(module == 9
#                 )
# 
# module_scores |> 
#   dplyr::filter(module %in% c('2', '1', '9'))
```


The "High SAINT score" modules were among the modules with significant enrichment against the background Proteins in the experiments;

However, many did not, *including TMEM's* and mostly because of substantial 
mixing between proteins scored as "noise" (low posterior probability of being a true interactor) and signal

### Sub-Module Analysis

Sub-module enrichment analysis on modules without any enrichment (to describe
themes of proteins within these modules)

```{r SubModule index}
"%notin%" <- Negate("%in%")

# index the modules without significant enrichment to describe sub-modules
tier2_modules <- c(seq(1:28) |> as.character())[
  which(c(seq(1:28) |> as.character()) %notin% c(global_enrichment_results_df |> 
          dplyr::distinct(module) |> 
          dplyr::pull()) == T)] 
```

```{r SubModule function}
# function to gather sub-modules for each module that did not return enrichment results
submodules_from_hclust <- function(
  hc_object,
  modules_df,
  n_sub_per_module = 3L
) {
  # number of existing modules
  n_modules <- length(unique(modules_df$module))

  # total number of clusters in the finer cut
  K_big <- n_modules * n_sub_per_module

  # finer cut of the same tree
  sub_labels_all <- stats::cutree(hc_object, k = K_big)

  # assemble into a tibble
  sub_df <- tibble::tibble(
    Protein   = names(sub_labels_all),
    sub_label = sub_labels_all
  )

  # attach existing module labels
  sub_df <- dplyr::inner_join(
    sub_df,
    modules_df,
    by = "Protein"
  )

  # define submodules as (module, sub_label) pairs
  submodules_df <- sub_df %>%
    dplyr::group_by(module, sub_label) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      submodule = paste0("sub", dplyr::cur_group_id())
    ) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::select(Protein, module, submodule)

  return(submodules_df)
}
```

```{r SubModule application}
submodules_df <- dplyr::filter(modules_df, module %in% tier2_modules)

submodules_df <- submodules_from_hclust(
  hc_object  = hc,
  modules_df = submodules_df,
  n_sub_per_module = 3L
)
```

```{r GSEA on SubModules}
submodule_enrichment_results <- submodules_df %>%
  dplyr::group_by(module, submodule) %>%
  dplyr::group_modify(~ {
    genes <- .x$Protein

    tibble::tibble(
      go_bp = list(get_GO(genes, ontology = "BP")),
      go_cc = list(get_GO(genes, ontology = "CC")),
      go_mf = list(get_GO(genes, ontology = "MF"))
    )
  }) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()
```

```{r Gather SubModule GSEA data}
submodule_enrichment_results_df <- submodule_enrichment_results |> 
    tidyr::pivot_longer(
      cols      = c(dplyr::contains("go_")),
      names_to  = "GO_Category",
      values_to = "values"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      GO_Category = gsub(GO_Category, pattern = 'go_', replacement = '') |> 
        stringr::str_to_upper(),
      GO_tbl = purrr::map(values, ~ as.data.frame(.x))
    ) |>
    tidyr::unnest(GO_tbl) |>
    dplyr::rename(
      GO_ID   = ID,
      GO_Term = Description
    ) |>
    dplyr::select(
      module,
      submodule,
      GO_Category,
      GO_Term,
      GO_ID,
      dplyr::everything(),
      -values
    )
```

```{r Assess TMEM SubModule}
submodule_enrichment_results_df |> 
  dplyr::filter(module == '12')
# TMEM's module not enriched

submodules_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(module == 12) %>%
  dplyr::count(submodule, name = "n_proteins")
# lots of proteins in the submodule

submodules_df |> 
  dplyr::filter(Protein == 'TMEM184B')
# but present in the submodules
```

```{r Find UnEnriched SubModules}
modules_with_submodule_enrichment <- tier2_modules
modules_without_submodule_enrichment <- generics::setdiff(modules_with_submodule_enrichment,
                                                          submodule_enrichment_results_df |> 
                                                            dplyr::distinct(module) |> 
                                                            dplyr::pull())
modules_with_submodule_enrichment <- submodule_enrichment_results_df |> 
  dplyr::distinct(module) |> 
  dplyr::pull()
```

```{r Collapse GO data}

collapsed_go <- global_enrichment_results_df |> 
  dplyr::group_by(module, GO_Category) |> 
  dplyr::summarize(
    theme = paste0(
      head(GO_Term[order(p.adjust)], 3),
      collapse = '; '
    ),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) |> 
  dplyr::full_join(
   submodule_enrichment_results_df |>
  dplyr::group_by(module, submodule, GO_Category) |> 
  dplyr::summarize(
    theme = paste0(
      head(GO_Term[order(p.adjust)], 3),
      collapse = "; "
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
) %>%
  dplyr::bind_rows(
    tibble::tibble(module = modules_without_submodule_enrichment)
  )
```

```{r GOTermSimilarityNetwork}

go_network <- global_enrichment_results_df |> 
  dplyr::full_join(submodule_enrichment_results_df) |> 
  dplyr::full_join(module_scores) |> 
  dplyr::mutate(Tier = dplyr::case_when(is.na(submodule) ~ 'Tier1',TRUE~'Tier2'),
                node_to = GO_Term,
                node_from = paste0(
                  
                  "Module", module,
                  ifelse(is.na(submodule),
                         '',
                         paste0('_SubModule',
                                submodule %>% 
                                  stringr::str_extract(., pattern = '\\d+')))),
                weight = -log10(p.adjust)) |> 
  dplyr::filter(weight > -log10(0.05))

go_network |> 
  dplyr::filter(module == 2)

g <- igraph::graph_from_data_frame(
  d = go_network[, c("node_from", "node_to",
                     "weight", "geneID", "p.adjust", "FoldEnrichment")],
  directed = TRUE,
  vertices =
    dplyr::bind_rows(
      go_network |> dplyr::transmute(name = node_from, score_mass, submodule),
      go_network |> dplyr::transmute(name = node_to,
                                     score_mass = NA_real_,
                                     submodule = NA_character_)
    ) |>
    dplyr::distinct(name, .keep_all = TRUE)
)
```


## Plots

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
  cat('## Plots {.tabset .tabset-pills .tabset-fade}\n')
}
```

### Hierarchical

#### Raw Counts Heatmap

```{r RawClusterZAll}
pheatmap::pheatmap(
  IPMS_z_ordered,
  color = grDevices::colorRampPalette(c('navy', 'white', 'darkgoldenrod'))((200)),
  cluster_rows = hc,
  cluster_cols = F,
  show_rownames = F,
  annotation_row = data.frame(Module = clusters_opt),
  annotation_colors = annotation_colors,
  angle_col = 0,
  use_raster = F,
  border_color = NA
)
```

#### Module Dot Plots

```{r RawModuleSubModuleDotPlotOfGOTerms}
global_enrichment_results_df |> 
  dplyr::full_join(submodule_enrichment_results_df) |> 
  dplyr::full_join(module_scores) |> 
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(GO_Term)) |> 
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(GO_Category)) |> 
  dplyr::mutate(
    Tier = dplyr::case_when(
      is.na(submodule) ~ paste0('Mod. ', module),
      TRUE ~ paste0('Mod. ', module,
                    ':\nsubmod. ',
                    gsub(submodule, pattern = 'sub', replacement = ''))
      ),
    is_enriched = dplyr::case_when(is.na(submodule) ~ 'Enriched',
                                   TRUE ~ 'Not Enriched')
    ) |> 
  dplyr::group_by(module) |>  
  dplyr::slice_head(n = 3) |>
  dplyr::arrange(p.adjust) |>
  ggplot2::ggplot(
    ggplot2::aes(
      x = -log10(p.adjust),
      y = forcats::fct_rev(forcats::fct_infreq(GO_Term, mean_score)),
      color = GO_Category,
      size = FoldEnrichment,
      alpha = mean_score,
      shape = is_enriched,
    )
  ) +
  ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = -log10(0.05),
                      linetype = 'dashed',
                      color = 'firebrick',
                      size = 1,
                      alpha = 0.5) +
  ggplot2::geom_point() +
  ggplot2::facet_grid(Tier ~ ., scales = 'free_y') +
  ggplot2::labs(title = 'GO Enrichment Across\nModules and SubModules\nin TMEM184B IPMS\nHierarchical Clustering',
                x = '-log10(Adj.P-val)',
                y = 'GO Term',
                alpha = 'Hierarchical\nSAINT\nScore',
                size = 'FoldEnrichment',
                color = 'GO Category',
                shape = 'IsEnrichedModule') +
  ggplot2::theme_bw() +
  ggplot2::theme(title = ggplot2::element_text(face = 'bold', size = 12),
                 axis.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = 'bold', size = 10),
                 strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = 'bold', size = 7), 
                 strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(color = 'black',
                                                          fill = 'white')) +
  ggplot2::guides(#shape = ggplot2::guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 4, shape = c(19, 17))),
                  color = ggplot2::guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 4, shape = 19, color = c('navy', 'darkgoldenrod3', 'firebrick'))),
                  alpha = ggplot2::guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 5, shape = 19)),
                  size = ggplot2::guide_legend(override.aes = list(shape = 19))
                  # size = 'none'
                  ) +
  ggplot2::scale_color_manual(values = c('navy', 'darkgoldenrod3', 'firebrick')) +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(values = c('navy', 'darkgoldenrod3', 'firebrick'))
```

#### GO Term Similarity Network

```{r GOTermSimilarityNetwork}
coords <- igraph::layout_with_fr(g)
coords <- coords*200

visNetwork::visNetwork(
  nodes = data.frame(
    id    = igraph::V(g)$name,
    label = igraph::V(g)$name,
    size = scales::rescale(
      (\(x){ x[is.na(x)] <- 0; x })(igraph::V(g)$score_mass),
      to = c(20, 60)
    ),
    x = coords[,1],
    y = coords[,2],
    group = base::interaction(
                        {
                          x <- igraph::V(g)$module
                          if (length(x) == 0) rep("", igraph::vcount(g)) else x
                        },
                        {
                          x <- igraph::V(g)$submodule
                          if (length(x) == 0) {
                            rep("", igraph::vcount(g))
                          } else {
                            ifelse(nzchar(x), x, igraph::V(g)$module)
                          }
                        },
                        drop = TRUE,
                        lex.order = TRUE
                      ),
    title = sapply(
      igraph::V(g),
      function(v) {
        incoming_edges <- igraph::incident(g, v, mode = "all")
        incoming_edges <- incoming_edges[
          igraph::ends(g, incoming_edges)[,2] == igraph::V(g)[v]$name
        ]
        raw_gene_strings <- igraph::E(g)[incoming_edges]$geneID
        raw_gene_strings <- unlist(raw_gene_strings, use.names = FALSE)

        genes <- raw_gene_strings |>
          as.character() |>
          (\(x) x[!is.na(x)])() |>
          strsplit("\\\\") |>
          unlist() |>
          (\(x) x[nzchar(x)])() |>
          unique()

        term <- igraph::V(g)[v]$name
        saint_value <- igraph::V(g)[v]$score_mass
        if (is.na(saint_value)) saint_value <- 0
        is_module <- nzchar(igraph::V(g)[v]$submodule)

        if (is_module) {
          paste0(
            "<div style='background-color:#ffffff;color:#1f4e79;",
            "padding:6px 10px;border-radius:6px;",
            "border:1px solid #1f4e79;font-size:12px;'>",
            "<b>", term, "</b><br>",
            "<b>SAINT mass:</b> ", signif(saint_value, 3), "<br>",
            if (length(genes) > 0)
              paste0("<u>Proteins:</u><br>", paste(genes, collapse = "<br>"))
            else "",
            "</div>"
          )
        } else {
          paste0(
            "<div style='background-color:#ffffff;color:#1f4e79;",
            "padding:6px 10px;border-radius:6px;",
            "border:1px solid #1f4e79;font-size:12px;'>",
            "<b>", term, "</b><br>",
            if (length(genes) > 0)
              paste0("<u>Proteins:</u><br>", paste(genes, collapse = "<br>"))
            else "",
            "</div>"
          )
        }
      }
    )
  ),
  edges = data.frame(
    from  = igraph::as_data_frame(g, "edges")$from,
    to    = igraph::as_data_frame(g, "edges")$to,
    value = igraph::E(g)$weight,
    title = vapply(
      seq_along(igraph::E(g)),
      function(i) {
        paste0(
          "<div style='background-color:#ffffff;color:#1f4e79;",
          "padding:6px 10px;border-radius:6px;",
          "border:1px solid #1f4e79;font-size:12px;'>",
          "<b>p.adjust:</b> ", signif(igraph::E(g)$p.adjust[i], 3), "<br>",
          "<b>weight:</b> ", signif(igraph::E(g)$weight[i], 3),
          "</div>"
        )
      },
      FUN.VALUE = character(1)
    )
  )
) |>
  # visNetwork::visNodes(color = NULL) |>
  visNetwork::visEdges(arrows = "to") |>
  visNetwork::visNodes(
    scaling = list(min = 5, max = 40),
    shadow  = TRUE
  ) |>
  visNetwork::visLayout(randomSeed = 1) |>
  visNetwork::visPhysics(enabled = FALSE) |>
  visNetwork::visInteraction(
    dragView = TRUE,
    zoomView = TRUE
  ) |>
  visNetwork::visOptions(nodesIdSelection = TRUE)
rm(coords)
```

Modules with enriched GO Terms are in blue; "sub-modules", modules with enough
mixture of disparate proteins and of widely different signal strength, were
re-enriched by their module, creating "sub-modules". *These are the non-blue colors.*

Modules **2**, **9**, **15**, and **26** are of interest, having terms in line with what might
be expected from a `TMEM` experiment, and having robust `Hierarchical SAINT` scores
(hover over Module/SubModule nodes).

`TMEM184B`, itself, was in Module **12**, which did *NOT* return any enriched GO Terms, likely
due to its membrane protein nature (and putative localization, where it touches 
a wide variety of proteins and organelles). This is common among TM protein IPMS.

#### GO Terms Mapped Back to the Hierarchical Heatmap

```{r}
# gaps_row = which(diff(clusters_opt) != 0)
# modules_df
# pheatmap::pheatmap(
#   IPMS_z_ordered,
#   color = grDevices::colorRampPalette(c('navy', 'white', 'darkgoldenrod'))(200),
#   cluster_rows = hc$order,
#   cluster_cols = FALSE,
#   show_rownames = FALSE,
#   annotation_row = data.frame(Module = clusters_opt),
#   annotation_colors = annotation_colors,
#   gaps_row = which(diff(clusters_opt) != 0),
#   angle_col = 0,
#   use_raster = FALSE,
#   border_color = NA
# )
```


#### Z-scored Counts

```{r Hierarchical Clustering Z, include = T, warning = F, message = F, echo = T}
d_Z <- as.dist(1 - cor(t(IPMS_mat_Z), method = "pearson"))
hc_Z <- hclust(d_Z, method = "average")
```
